top_signals.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
top_signals.summary:
CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation.

  • CVE-2022-0492 Linux Kernel Improper Authentication Vulnerability
  • CVE-2025-48595 Android Framework Integer Overflow Vulnerability

These types of vulnerabilities are a frequent attack vector for malicious cyber actors and pose significant risks to the federal enterprise.

Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 22-01: Reducing the Significant Risk of Known Exploited Vulnerabilities established the KEV Catalog as a living list of known Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) that carry significant risk to the federal enterprise. BOD 22-01 requires Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies to remediate identified vulnerabilities by the due date to protect FCEB networks against active threats. See the BOD 22-01 Fact Sheet for more information.

Although BOD 22-01 only applies to FCEB agencies, CISA strongly urges all organizations to reduce their exposure to cyberattacks by prioritizing timely remediation of KEV Catalog vulnerabilities as part of their vulnerability management practice. CISA will continue to add vulnerabilities to the catalog that meet the specified criteria.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Potential liquidity or banking-system instability affecting financial conditions.

top_signals.title: Federal Reserve Board announces final rule that establishes data standards for certain information collections
top_signals.summary: Federal Reserve Board announces final rule that establishes data standards for certain information collections
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Nominations opening soon for 2027 FreightTech Awards
top_signals.summary:
FreightWaves will host its annual Future of Freight Festival (F3) in Chattanooga, Tennessee, culminating in the prestigious 2027 FreightTech Awards dinner.

The post Nominations opening soon for 2027 FreightTech Awards appeared first on FreightWaves.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Paris defense show may be last chance to buy before war, CEO says
top_signals.summary: Surging defense spending means Eurosatory is bigger than ever this year, with the number of exhibitors rising 30% to more than 2,600, per the organizer.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Spot bitcoin ETFs snap five-day outflow streak with $85.8 million Friday inflow as ether funds keep sliding
top_signals.summary: BlackRock's IBIT led Friday's inflows at $57.7 million, with Fidelity's FBTC adding $18.0 million, while no fund reported a net outflow on the day.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Major crypto exchanges cancel SpaceX IPO allocations, promising refunds
top_signals.summary: Elon Musk’s SpaceX completed its landmark IPO on the Nasdaq on Friday, but crypto users seeking tokenized exposure to the IPO were left empty-handed after allocations fell through.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Anthropic suspends access to Fable 5, Mythos 5, citing US directive
top_signals.summary: Anthropic has abruptly disabled its flagship AI models after a US government directive citing national security concerns.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
